attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Clean. Let me do a final review of the updated `TopBar.tsx` right section to make sure everything looks right:

lastPrompt: [Image #1] this area here in the top right is bothering me. looks hideous and unsure what each of them an do; and even some bugs in the layouting. make this as good as the rest of the app...

aiTitle: Improve top right area layout and styling

content.text:
Everything looks good. The changes are:

**What changed:**

1. **`TopBar.tsx`** — The two context selectors are now proper first-class props (`perspectiveOptions/Value/onChange` and `planStyleOptions/Value/onChange`). They render as a `TopBarSelect` component — a styled `<select>` with `appearance-none` + an absolute chevron icon, matching the app's border/surface/shadow tokens. A subtle `1px` separator bar divides them from the icon buttons. The peer avatars now use `−space-x-1` overlap stacking (like avatar groups in real apps). The own avatar is now a rounded-full pill with `accent` tint instead of a square. The overall gap is tightened from `gap-2` to `gap-1.5`.

2. **`RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`** — The awkward external `<div className="ml-2 mr-3 ...">` holding the raw `<select>` elements is gone. The two selectors are now passed as props to `TopBar`. Added `PLAN_STYLE_OPTIONS` constant.

3. **`i18n.ts`** — Added `perspective` ("Discipline") and `planStyle` ("Plan style") keys in both `en` and `de` locales for the `aria-label` and `title` attributes.

content.text: Clear diagnosis: `onHamburgerClick` and `onSettings` are never passed, and the left-rail collapsed state is trapped inside `AppShell` with no way out. I need to lift that state and wire both buttons.

I'll add controlled rail-state props to `AppShell`, then lift the state into `RedesignedWorkspace` so the hamburger can drive it. Settings will open the cheatsheet (the closest settings-like surface the app has).

lines: /** Initial collapsed state for the right rail. Defaults to false. */

* Used by tests to scope the listeners. */
+      const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(leftCollapsed) : updater;

content.text: Now wire `onHamburgerClick` and `onSettings` in the TopBar call, and pass the controlled rail state to AppShell:

input.old_string:
<AppShell
        topBar={
          <div className="flex w-full flex-col">
            <div className="flex w-full items-center">
              <TopBar
                mode={mode}
                onModeChange={handleModeChange}
                projectName="BIM AI seed"
                projectNameRef={projectNameRef}
                onProjectNameClick={() => setProjectMenuOpen((v) => !v)}
                theme={theme}
                onThemeToggle={handleThemeToggle}
                onCommandPalette={() => setPaletteOpen(true)}
                collaboratorsCount={Object.keys(presencePeers).length || undefined}
                onCollaboratorsClick={() => setCommentsOpen((v) => !v)}
                peers={Object.values(presencePeers)}
                avatarInitials={userDisplayName ? userDisplayName.slice(0, 2).toUpperCase() : 'BA'}
                perspectiveOptions={PERSPECTIVE_OPTIONS}
                perspectiveValue={perspectiveId}
                onPerspectiveChange={(v) => setPerspectiveId(v as PerspectiveId)}
                planStyleOptions={PLAN_STYLE_OPTIONS}
                planStyleValue={planPresentationPreset}
                onPlanStyleChange={(v) => setPlanPresentationPreset(v as 'default' | 'opening_focus' | 'room_scheme')}
              />

lastPrompt: [Image #2] [Image #3] there are still several dead buttons. why?

aiTitle: Improve top right area layout and styling

content.text: Now check TypeScript compiles clean:

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
Clean. Here's what was fixed:

**Root cause:** Both `onHamburgerClick` and `onSettings` were never passed to `TopBar` in `RedesignedWorkspace`, and the left-rail collapsed state was trapped inside `AppShell` with no way for outside callers to drive it.

**Fixes:**
1. **`AppShell`** — Added optional controlled props `leftCollapsed` / `onLeftCollapsedChange`. When provided they take over; when absent, AppShell falls back to its internal state (existing behavior for tests/default usage unchanged).

2. **`RedesignedWorkspace`** — Added `leftRailCollapsed` state, passed it to `AppShell` as controlled state, and wired `onHamburgerClick` to toggle it. The `[` keyboard shortcut continues to work because AppShell's key handler now calls through the same setter.

3. **Settings button** — Wired `onSettings` to open the cheatsheet modal (`setCheatsheetOpen(true)`), which is the closest thing to an app-settings surface the app currently has (keyboard shortcuts, theme, language).

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(topbar): wire dead buttons + move context selects in-band

- Perspective and plan-style selects moved from an external div into
  TopBar as styled TopBarSelect components (appearance-none + chevron
  icon, matching app design tokens); removes the layout hack in
  RedesignedWorkspace
- Peer avatars now use −space-x-1 overlap stacking; own avatar is
  rounded-full with accent tint
- AppShell gains optional controlled leftCollapsed/onLeftCollapsedChange
  props so callers can drive rail state without breaking uncontrolled use
- Hamburger button wired to toggle the left rail (was dead)
- Settings button wired to open the cheatsheet modal (was dead)
- i18n: add topbar.perspective + topbar.planStyle keys (en + de)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
